text:
*[Bug 19050] New: float-to-int overflow in R parser*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19050>

            Bug ID: 19050
           Summary: float-to-int overflow in R parser
           Product: R
           Version: R-devel (trunk)
          Hardware: Other
                OS: Linux
            Status: NEW
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5
         Component: Low-level
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:kevinushey@gmail.com|kevinushey@gmail.com>

Created attachment 3672
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3672&action=edit>
Patch.

When R parses an integer literal suffixed with L whose value exceeds the range
of int, the function NumericValue() in src/main/gram.c performs an unchecked
(int) a cast at line 4973 where a is the double result of R_atof(). If a is
outside [INT_MIN, INT_MAX], this cast is undefined behavior per C11 6.3.1.4.

R already detects this case and falls back to a numeric value (the a !=
(double) b check at line 4978), but the UB has already occurred by line 4973
before that check runs. In practice the value of b is indeterminate after the
overflow, so the comparison may or may not work depending on the compiler.

Reproducer:

parse(text = "99999999999999999999L")

Or with a hex literal:

parse(text = "0XEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEL")

Both trigger the following UBSAN diagnostic when R is built with
-fsanitize=undefined:

gram.c:4973:10: runtime error: 1e+20 is outside the range of representable
values of type 'int'

---

> parse(text = "0XEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEL")
/home/kevin/r-devel/r-afl/r-svn/src/main/gram.c:4973:10: runtime error:
1.18314e+30 is outside the range of representable values of type 'int'
    #0 0x7f6f8d153e3f in NumericValue
/home/kevin/r-devel/r-afl/r-svn/src/main/gram.c:4973:10
    #1 0x7f6f8d1502a9 in token
/home/kevin/r-devel/r-afl/r-svn/src/main/gram.c:5854:28
    #2 0x7f6f8d1502a9 in token_
/home/kevin/r-devel/r-afl/r-svn/src/main/gram.c:6092:15
    #3 0x7f6f8d13e23e in yylex
/home/kevin/r-devel/r-afl/r-svn/src/main/gram.c:6118:11
    #4 0x7f6f8d13e23e in Rf_yyparse
/home/kevin/r-devel/r-afl/r-svn/src/main/gram.c:2186:16
    #5 0x7f6f8d16722b in R_Parse1
/home/kevin/r-devel/r-afl/r-svn/src/main/gram.c:4043:12
    #6 0x7f6f8d168b64 in R_Parse
/home/kevin/r-devel/r-afl/r-svn/src/main/gram.c:4202:9
    #7 0x7f6f8d16d9d6 in R_ParseVector
/home/kevin/r-devel/r-afl/r-svn/src/main/gram.c:4290:12
    #8 0x7f6f8d40e750 in do_parse
/home/kevin/r-devel/r-afl/r-svn/src/main/source.c:286:6
    #9 0x7f6f8d07bb65 in bcEval_loop
/home/kevin/r-devel/r-afl/r-svn/src/main/eval.c:8132:14
    #10 0x7f6f8d060983 in bcEval
/home/kevin/r-devel/r-afl/r-svn/src/main/eval.c:7515:16
    #11 0x7f6f8d05e8dd in Rf_eval
/home/kevin/r-devel/r-afl/r-svn/src/main/eval.c:1167:8
    #12 0x7f6f8d0fdffd in R_execClosure
/home/kevin/r-devel/r-afl/r-svn/src/main/eval.c:2389:22
    #13 0x7f6f8d0fcb4a in applyClosure_core
/home/kevin/r-devel/r-afl/r-svn/src/main/eval.c:2302:16
    #14 0x7f6f8d05f440 in Rf_applyClosure
/home/kevin/r-devel/r-afl/r-svn/src/main/eval.c:2324:16
    #15 0x7f6f8d05f440 in Rf_eval
/home/kevin/r-devel/r-afl/r-svn/src/main/eval.c:1280:12
    #16 0x7f6f8d1d36f6 in Rf_ReplIteration
/home/kevin/r-devel/r-afl/r-svn/src/main/main.c:264:15
    #17 0x7f6f8d1d77a0 in R_ReplConsole
/home/kevin/r-devel/r-afl/r-svn/src/main/main.c:317:11
    #18 0x7f6f8d1d75cc in run_Rmainloop
/home/kevin/r-devel/r-afl/r-svn/src/main/main.c:1235:5
    #19 0x7f6f8d1d7892 in Rf_mainloop
/home/kevin/r-devel/r-afl/r-svn/src/main/main.c:1242:5
    #20 0x0000004e9e8c in main
/home/kevin/r-devel/r-afl/r-svn/src/main/Rmain.c:29:5
    #21 0x7f6f8c8e15b4 in __libc_start_call_main (/lib64/libc.so.6+0x35b4)
(BuildId: ff0267465bc3d76e21003b3bc5598fd5ee63e261)
    #22 0x7f6f8c8e1667 in __libc_start_main@GLIBC_2.2.5
(/lib64/libc.so.6+0x3667) (BuildId: ff0267465bc3d76e21003b3bc5598fd5ee63e261)

            Status: NEW
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5

Patch.

When R parses an integer literal suffixed with L whose value exceeds the range
of int, the function NumericValue() in src/main/gram.c performs an unchecked
(int) a cast at line 4973 where a is the double result of R_atof(). If a is
outside [INT_MIN, INT_MAX], this cast is undefined behavior per C11 6.3.1.4.

R already detects this case and falls back to a numeric value (the a !=
(double) b check at line 4978), but the UB has already occurred by line 4973
before that check runs. In practice the value of b is indeterminate after the
overflow, so the comparison may or may not work depending on the compiler.

Reproducer:

Or with a hex literal:

parse(text = "0XEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEL")

Both trigger the following UBSAN diagnostic when R is built with
-fsanitize=undefined:

gram.c

10: runtime error: 1e+20 is outside the range of representable
values of type 'int'

*[Bug 19049] btpe implementation rbinom has incorrect signs for error terms in Stirling approximation*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19049>

Martin Maechler (<mailto:maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch|maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
             Status|UNCONFIRMED                 |CONFIRMED
     Ever confirmed|0                           |1
                 CC|                            |maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch

--- Comment #1 from Martin Maechler (<mailto:maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch|maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch>) ---
Makes a lot of sense, notably also the link to the gsl (C library) source code
with its extended comments.
There, it is mentioned that they did not find *one* single case where the
change made a difference.
Maybe that's easier to find using R simulations .. ?

It's good you found this here too, and the original double typo in the 1988
publication code will finally be fixed in other FOSS projects, too.

text:
*[Bug 17702] Assignment to data.frame column (`$<-` or `[[<-`) should convert POSIXlt to POSIXct*
https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=17702

Gabriel Becker (gabembecker@gmail.com) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |gabembecker@gmail.com

--- Comment #1 from Gabriel Becker (gabembecker@gmail.com) ---
I can confirm that this behavior remains in a recent R-devel.

This appears to be happening because `data.frame` converts its arguments to
data.frames before combining them, which is where the conversion from `POSIXlt`
to `POSIXct` is occuring.

```
> df <- as.data.frame(as.POSIXlt(runif(10, 1.4e9, 1.7e9), origin='1970-1-1'))
> lapply(df, class)
$`as.POSIXlt(runif(10, 1.4e+09, 1.7e+09), origin = "1970-1-1")`
[1] "POSIXct" "POSIXt" 
```

According to the documentation for `$<-.data.frame`:

> There is no ‘data.frame’ method for ‘$’, so ‘x$name’ uses the default method which treats ‘x’ as a list (with partial matching of column names if the match is unique, see ‘Extract’).  The replacement method (for ‘$’) checks ‘value’ for the correct number of rows, and replicates it if necessary. 

This matches the actual code for `$<-.data.frame` which strips the class off of
`x`, checks the number of rows in `value`, reps `value` out as needed, assigns
into `x` while it's still a list, then reapply's `x`'s class. 

Thus this is working as documented, at least. 

That said I'm sympathetic to wanting the behaviors to match. We don't have dual
dispatch for S3 methods, so the only ways I can think of to implement this
change (if desired) would be 

1. have `$<-.data.frame` convert `value` to a data.frame before shoving it into
`x`, or 
2. hard-code a special case for value being `POSIXlt` to convert it to
`POSIXct`.

Approach 1 seems *way* too fundamental a shift for this level of behavior
change, and the amount of testing we'd need to do to make sure this didn't
break anything else seems.. daunting to say the least.

Approach 2 is doable, if a bit hacky, if desired. Happy to prepare a patch,
though I suspect if a member of R-core wants to put that change in it'd be
easier for them to just do it themselves.

text:
*[Bug 15715] make call() more flexible*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=15715>

Gabriel Becker (<mailto:gabembecker@gmail.com|gabembecker@gmail.com>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
     Ever confirmed|1                           |0
                 CC|                            |gabembecker@gmail.com
             Status|NEW                         |UNCONFIRMED

--- Comment #4 from Gabriel Becker (<mailto:gabembecker@gmail.com|gabembecker@gmail.com>) ---
IIUC the only difference between leaving it a special and making it a builtin
is that for invalid first arguments that throw that error, the remaining
arguments currently are not evaluated (because as Michael said they are
evaluated anyway but it's after the error).

Changing it to a builtin would mean they are evaluated right away, and thus
will be evaluated even in the case of an invalid first argument.

I suppose given that knowledge I could write code that would give different
results before and after the change, but it would be pretty gross and highly
targeted. 

That said, maybe the byte code compiler would (or would need to) handle it
differently. I don't know much about what it is doing.

Seems like it'd be easy to put in support for the symbols, as that was
uncontroversial, but we've gone 12 years (almost exactly at time of writing)
without the change so maybe its not necessary and could the issue could be
closed?
